title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Gwendolyn (Gwenith) and Jules visit Dr. Kennedy at Highcliffe Manor to discuss Gwendolyn's past. Gwendolyn reveals she has purchased a mountain-side villa in Dealmore, which Dr. Kennedy identifies as St. Catherine's Manor, the house where Gwendolyn previously lived. Gwendolyn explains her motive for returning to the area is a sense of familiarity and a feeling that the place is home, despite her hazy memories.

Seeking information regarding her father and her sister, Helen, Gwendolyn questions Dr. Kennedy. He recounts that Helen returned from India on the same ship as Gwendolyn's father, Kelvin Hardy. Although Kelvin was a single father at the time, they married in London and lived in Deal. However, less than a year later, Helen ran away with another man, whom Kennedy suggests may have been a married Roman Catholic. Kennedy notes that Kelvin did not want a divorce. Regarding Gwendolyn's father, Kennedy suggests he sent her to New Zealand due to pressure from her mother's relatives following the breakdown of his second marriage.

When Gwendolyn asks about her father's death, Kennedy claims Kelvin's health was poor and that he died in a nursing home on the East Coast two years after leaving Dilworth. Kennedy avoids providing the specific location of the nursing home or the grave, dismissively arguing that focusing on the past is a mistake. Kennedy further reveals that he was Helen's half-brother and had helped her through past troubles before she married Walter Fane in India. Gwendolyn concludes that her primary goal in contacting him was to learn more about her father.
